name: the Brinefolk

saveActions.text: Engineer a covered cistern so their pools stop losing the last water to the sky.
Raiders know what the pools are worth. Stand between.

exploitActions.text: Pump the pools and take a season of god-water in a week. The pumps are Ur-Noth's design, incidentally.
Lend them grain against next year's water. There will be less water every year. You both know it.

reactions.saved: Your gods drink well forever — a standing fair-price god-water line, and Brinefolk lamps on the rim at the end.
reactions.extinguished: Dry pools. The best offerings in the game, gone from the world. Every priest you ever host will eventually ask what happened here.
reactions.grimWorksInSight: Seeing the drain-pumps in your village ends fair trade permanently — they recognize the design.

lore: Salt-veined descendants who survived by drinking god-charged brine. They keep the last pools, and the Vigor economy runs through how you treat them.
